title: Personal event management: domain, application, and API for user-owned events
body:
## Issue description

Currently, all event operations are scoped to a group â€” events are created within a group context, listed per group, and accessed via group-prefixed API routes (`/api/v1/groups/:groupId/events`). Users cannot manage events independently of groups.

This issue covers building personal event management so users can create, view, edit, and delete their own events without requiring a group context. Personal events are private by default and only visible to the owner until explicitly shared.

### Current state
- `CreateEventUseCase` requires `groupId` and seeds all group members as invitees
- `Event` domain entity has a required `groupId` field
- All event API routes are nested under `/api/v1/groups/:groupId/events`
- `event.router.ts` validates `groupId` parameter on every request
- Event capture pipeline (`CaptureTextUseCase`, etc.) requires `groupId`

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### 1. Domain layer changes
- Update `Event` entity: `groupId` becomes optional, add `ownerId` (required)
- Add `PersonalEvent` concept or extend existing `Event` to differentiate personal vs. shared
- Update `CreateEventData` to make `groupId` optional and `inviteeIds` optional (personal events have no invitees by default)
- Ensure domain rules enforce: only the owner can manage their personal events

### 2. Application layer â€” new use cases
- **CreatePersonalEventUseCase** â€” Create an event owned by the calling user, no group context required. No invitations seeded.
- **ListMyEventsUseCase** â€” List all events owned by the calling user (personal events + events they created in groups). Support date range filtering.
- **GetPersonalEventUseCase** â€” Fetch a personal event by ID (owner-only access)
- **UpdatePersonalEventUseCase** â€” Update a personal event (owner-only)
- **DeletePersonalEventUseCase** â€” Delete a personal event (owner-only). Only allowed if event is not shared, or sharing is revoked first.

### 3. Infrastructure layer
- Update `KnexEventRepository` to support queries without `groupId`
- Add query methods for user-owned events with date range filtering
- Ensure existing group-scoped queries continue to work

### 4. Presentation layer â€” new API endpoints

**Personal event endpoints (new):**
| Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
|--------|----------|----------|
| POST | `/api/v1/events` | Create personal event |
| GET | `/api/v1/events` | List my events (with optional date range query params) |
| GET | `/api/v1/events/:eventId` | Get personal event detail |
| PATCH | `/api/v1/events/:eventId` | Update personal event |
| DELETE | `/api/v1/events/:eventId` | Delete personal event |

**Request/response contracts:**
- `POST /api/v1/events` body: `{ title, startAt, endAt?, description? }` â†’ returns `Event` (201)
- `GET /api/v1/events?from=ISO&to=ISO` â†’ returns `Event[]` (200)
- Authorization: all endpoints require authenticated user; all operations are owner-only

### 5. Event capture pipeline update
- Update capture pipeline to support personal event creation when `groupId` is not provided
- When `groupId` is omitted, create a personal event (no invitations)
- When `groupId` is provided, retain existing behavior (create group-scoped event with invitations)
- Update draft model to handle personal events

### 6. Authorization rules
- Personal events: only the owner can view, edit, or delete
- No other user can see a personal event unless it is explicitly shared to a group they belong to (covered by the sharing issue)
- Personal events must not appear in any group event listing
- Personal events must not leak existence to non-owners through any API response

---

## Additional notes

- Depends on: Database schema migration for personal event ownership
- Existing group-scoped event endpoints (`/api/v1/groups/:groupId/events`) should continue to work during transition
- The `event-management` module structure should be extended, not replaced
- Follow clean architecture: domain â†’ application â†’ infrastructure â†’ presentation
- Follow existing naming conventions (e.g., `CreatePersonalEventUseCase`, `PersonalEventPolicy`)

### Related files
- `src/modules/event-management/domain/event.ts`
- `src/modules/event-management/application/create-event.usecase.ts`
- `src/modules/event-management/presentation/event.router.ts`
- `src/modules/event-capture/` (capture pipeline)
- `client/src/api/events.ts`

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `Event` domain entity supports optional `groupId` and required `ownerId`
- [ ] Users can create personal events via `POST /api/v1/events` without any group context
- [ ] Users can list their own events via `GET /api/v1/events` with optional date range filtering
- [ ] Users can view, update, and delete their personal events
- [ ] Personal events are invisible to all other users (no leakage via any endpoint)
- [ ] Existing group-scoped event APIs continue to function
- [ ] Event capture pipeline creates personal events when `groupId` is omitted
- [ ] Unit tests cover all new domain rules and policies
- [ ] Integration tests cover personal event persistence
- [ ] API tests cover all new endpoints (happy path, auth, validation, authorization)
- [ ] Authorization/privacy tests verify owner-only access and non-leakage
- [ ] CI pipeline passes
